6	You may obtain a copy of the License at
7	
8	    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
9	
10	Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
11	distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
12	WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
13	See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
14	limitations under the License.
15	*/
16	
17	package v1alpha1
18	
19	import (
20		metav1 "k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1"
21	)
22	
23	// SecretRefs declares the K8s Secret names that carry credentials.
24	// Per AUTH-01, populated in Phase 3; Phase 1 ships the field shape only.
25	type SecretRefs struct {
26		// AnthropicAPIKey is the Secret name carrying the LLM API key.
27		// +optional
28		AnthropicAPIKey string `json:"anthropicAPIKey,omitempty"`
29		// GitCredentials is the Secret name carrying git push credentials (PAT or SSH key).
30		// +optional
31		GitCredentials string `json:"gitCredentials,omitempty"`
32	}
33	
34	// ModelSelection picks per-level model identifiers.
35	// Phase 1 ships the field shape; Phase 2+ consumes.
36	type ModelSelection struct {
37		// +optional
38		Milestone string `json:"milestone,omitempty"`
39		// +optional
40		Phase string `json:"phase,omitempty"`
41		// +optional
42		Plan string `json:"plan,omitempty"`
43		// +optional
44		Task string `json:"task,omitempty"`
45	}
46	
47	// GatePolicy is one of "auto" | "approve" | "pause" — per-level human gate.
48	// Phase 1 ships the field shape; Phase 4 consumes.
49	// +kubebuilder:validation:Enum=auto;approve;pause
50	type GatePolicy string
51	
52	// Gates declares per-level gate policy. Phase 1 ships field; Phase 4 wires.
53	type Gates struct {
54		// +optional
55		Milestone GatePolicy `json:"milestone,omitempty"`
56		// +optional
57		Phase GatePolicy `json:"phase,omitempty"`
58		// +optional
59		Plan GatePolicy `json:"plan,omitempty"`
60		// +optional
61		Task GatePolicy `json:"task,omitempty"`
62		// +optional
63		PauseBetweenWaves bool `json:"pauseBetweenWaves,omitempty"`
64	}
65	
66	// RouteSpec is a (method, path-prefix) tuple used to extend the credproxy
67	// upstream allowlist for a Project (Phase 04.1 P4.2). The credproxy sidecar
68	// always enforces the hardcoded baseline (POST /v1/messages and
69	// POST /v1/messages/count_tokens); RouteSpec entries are additive extensions,
70	// never replacements.
71	type RouteSpec struct {
72		// Method is the HTTP method. Currently only GET and POST are accepted.
73		// +kubebuilder:validation:Enum=GET;POST
74		Method string `json:"method"`
75	
76		// PathPrefix is the URL path prefix. Must start with "/v1/" — paths
77		// outside the Anthropic API surface are rejected at admission.
78		// +kubebuilder:validation:Pattern=`^/v1/[a-zA-Z0-9_/-]+$`
79		PathPrefix string `json:"pathPrefix"`
80	}
81	
82	// ProviderConfig configures one LLM provider (Phase 2+).
83	type ProviderConfig struct {
84		// Name is the provider identifier (only "anthropic" is supported in v1).
85		// +kubebuilder:validation:Enum=anthropic
86		Name string `json:"name"`
87	
88		// RequestsPerMinute optionally caps API requests per minute for this provider.
89		// +optional
90		RequestsPerMinute *int32 `json:"requestsPerMinute,omitempty"`
91	
92		// TokensPerMinute optionally caps token throughput per minute for this provider.
93		// +optional
94		TokensPerMinute *int32 `json:"tokensPerMinute,omitempty"`
95	
96		// AllowedRoutes optionally extends the hardcoded credproxy allowlist
97		// (POST /v1/messages + POST /v1/messages/count_tokens) with additional
98		// (method, path-prefix) tuples. Use this to grant the subagent access
99		// to newly-released LLM endpoints (Files API, Search Tools, prompt
100		// caching) without rebuilding the credproxy image.
101		// Hardcoded safe defaults ALWAYS apply — operator additions are
102		// additive, never restrictive. Admin/billing paths are rejected at
103		// admission (defense in depth).
104		// +optional
105		AllowedRoutes []RouteSpec `json:"allowedRoutes,omitempty"`
106	}
107	
108	// BudgetConfig declares cost/token caps for the Project (Phase 2+).
109	type BudgetConfig struct {
110		// AbsoluteCapCents is the hard spending limit in USD cents for the project lifetime.
111		// +kubebuilder:validation:Minimum=0
112		AbsoluteCapCents int64 `json:"absoluteCapCents"`
113	
114		// RollingWindowCapCents caps spending over the rolling window defined by
115		// BudgetStatus.WindowStart and BudgetConfig.RollingWindowDuration. When
116		// the window elapses, ProjectReconciler.handleBudgetGate resets
117		// CostSpentCents + TokensSpent and advances WindowStart.
118		// +optional
119		RollingWindowCapCents int64 `json:"rollingWindowCapCents,omitempty"`
120	
121		// RollingWindowDuration is the window length over which
122		// RollingWindowCapCents applies. Default: 24h when unset. Must be >= 1h
123		// to prevent inadvertent denial-of-dispatch via tiny windows.
124		// Validation: enforced semantically in ProjectReconciler (metav1.Duration
125		// is a struct; controller-gen Pattern markers require a string type).
126		// +optional
127		RollingWindowDuration *metav1.Duration `json:"rollingWindowDuration,omitempty"`
128	}
129	
130	// PlanAdmissionConfig controls file-touch policy during plan validation (Phase 2+).
131	type PlanAdmissionConfig struct {
132		// FileTouchMode determines how file-touch mismatches are handled:
133		//   "strict" — reject plans whose file touches deviate from declarations.
134		//   "warn"   — admit but annotate the mismatch on Plan.Status.
135		// +kubebuilder:validation:Enum=strict;warn
136		// +optional
137		FileTouchMode string `json:"fileTouchMode,omitempty"`
138	}
139	
140	// SubagentConfig declares per-Project provider+model selection (Phase 3 D-C2).
141	//
142	// Resolution chain at dispatch time (in each reconciler before calling
143	// Dispatcher.Run): Spec.Subagent.Levels.{level}.{image,model} → Spec.Subagent.{image,model}
144	// → Helm-chart default. Vendor + model are orthogonal axes — Image picks the
145	// vendor (via the container image's bundled Subagent impl); Model picks the
146	// model identifier passed to that vendor's CLI/SDK.
147	type SubagentConfig struct {
148		// Image is the default subagent image reference for all levels.
149		// Example: "ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-claude-subagent:v0.1.0".
150		// +optional
151		Image string `json:"image,omitempty"`
152	
153		// Model is the default model identifier for all levels.
154		// Example: "claude-sonnet-4-6" (anthropic), "o1-mini" (openai).
155		// +optional
156		Model string `json:"model,omitempty"`
157	
158		// Levels carries per-level overrides; any subset is valid.
159		// +optional
160		Levels LevelOverrides `json:"levels,omitempty"`
161	}
162	
163	// LevelOverrides carries per-level provider/model overrides (Phase 3 D-C2).
164	// Each pointer is nil when no override is set for that level.
165	type LevelOverrides struct {
166		// Milestone optionally overrides settings for the milestone planner.
167		// +optional
168		Milestone *LevelConfig `json:"milestone,omitempty"`
169	
170		// Phase optionally overrides settings for the phase planner.
171		// +optional
172		Phase *LevelConfig `json:"phase,omitempty"`
173	
174		// Plan optionally overrides settings for the plan planner.
175		// +optional
176		Plan *LevelConfig `json:"plan,omitempty"`
177	
178		// Task optionally overrides settings for the task executor.
179		// +optional
180		Task *LevelConfig `json:"task,omitempty"`
181	}
182	
183	// LevelConfig declares the per-level subagent override (Phase 3 D-C2).
184	type LevelConfig struct {
185		// Model identifier for this level (e.g., "claude-haiku-4-5").
186		// +optional
187		Model string `json:"model,omitempty"`
188	
189		// Params carries per-vendor tuning passthrough (temperature, thinking
190		// budget, etc.). Validation lives in the provider's Subagent impl.
191		// +optional
192		Params map[string]string `json:"params,omitempty"`
193	
194		// Image override is schema-present-but-not-enforced in v1.0 (deferred
195		// to v1.x per CONTEXT.md "Deferred Ideas" — per-level cross-vendor
196		// image overrides). Set this in v1.0 only if the consumer is prepared
197		// to ignore it.
198		// +optional
199		Image string `json:"image,omitempty"`
200	}
201	
202	// GitConfig declares the per-Project git target + creds (Phase 3 D-B6).
203	// HTTPS+PAT is the primary v1.0 path; SSH is documented with host-key caveats
204	// per REQUIREMENTS.md ART-05 but defaults to HTTPS+PAT.
205	type GitConfig struct {
206		// RepoURL is the URL of the target git remote. Supports http:// and https://
207		// (pure-Go go-git transport, production default) and SSH
208		// (git@host:owner/repo.git, documented with host-key caveats).
209		// file:// is NOT supported: go-git's file:// transport shells out to a
210		// system git binary absent from production images.
211		// +kubebuilder:validation:Pattern=`^(https?://|git@).+`
212		RepoURL string `json:"repoURL"`
213	
214		// CredsSecretRef is the K8s Secret name (same-namespace) carrying the
215		// HTTPS PAT in data key GIT_PAT. Cross-namespace Secret refs are NOT
216		// permitted in v1.0 (threat-mitigation deferred to push Job — plan 03-06).
217		CredsSecretRef string `json:"credsSecretRef"`
218	
219		// LeaksConfigRef is an optional ConfigMap name carrying gitleaks rule
220		// overrides (D-B3). Default gitleaks rules are embedded in the push image
221		// when this is empty.
222		// +optional
223		LeaksConfigRef string `json:"leaksConfigRef,omitempty"`
224	}
225	
226	// GitStatus records the per-Project git push state (Phase 3 D-B6).
227	//
228	// One branch per Project lifetime: BranchName is fixed at Project creation
229	// as "tide/run-<project-name>-<unix-epoch>" (Unix epoch keeps refnames
230	// colon-free per RFC 3987 refname rules). LastPushedSHA + LeaseFailureCount
231	// drive the --force-with-lease push contract: each push uses lastPushedSHA
232	// as the lease, increments leaseFailureCount on rejection, and resets to 0
233	// on success.
234	type GitStatus struct {
235		// BranchName is the lifetime branch fixed at Project creation.
236		// Format: "tide/run-<project>-<unix-epoch>".
237		// +optional
238		BranchName string `json:"branchName,omitempty"`
239	
240		// LastPushedSHA is the head SHA recorded on the most recent successful push.
241		// Used as the lease for subsequent `git push --force-with-lease`.
242		// +optional
243		LastPushedSHA string `json:"lastPushedSHA,omitempty"`
244	
245		// LeaseFailureCount tallies consecutive push lease rejections; reset to 0
246		// on successful push. Reconciler halts (Phase=PushLeaseFailed) when this
247		// count exceeds the configured retry budget.
248		// +optional
249		LeaseFailureCount int32 `json:"leaseFailureCount,omitempty"`
250	}
251	
252	// BudgetStatus records running spend tallies for the Project (Phase 2+).
253	// PERSIST-02 / Pitfall 4: this is a TALLY object, not an aggregate schedule.
254	// The PERSIST-02 denylist (enforced by `make verify-no-aggregates`) does not
255	// apply to this struct — it carries only spend counters (tokensSpent,
256	// costSpentCents, windowStart), not a derived execution plan.
257	type BudgetStatus struct {
258		// TokensSpent is the cumulative token count spent since WindowStart.
259		// +optional
260		TokensSpent int64 `json:"tokensSpent,omitempty"`
261	
262		// CostSpentCents is the cumulative cost in USD cents since WindowStart.
263		// +optional
264		CostSpentCents int64 `json:"costSpentCents,omitempty"`
265	
266		// WindowStart marks the beginning of the current rolling budget window.
267		// +optional
268		WindowStart *metav1.Time `json:"windowStart,omitempty"`
269	}
270	
271	// BoundaryPushStatus records the bounded auto-retry state of the project-level
272	// boundary push (debug defect #13b). The boundary push lands the already-
273	// integrated run branch on the remote AFTER the Project reaches Complete; it is
274	// observability + bounded recovery, NEVER a gate on Complete.
275	//
276	// PERSIST-02 / Pitfall 4: this is a per-project retry tally object, NOT an
277	// aggregate schedule. Attempts is re-derived from .status on every reconcile so
278	// the bounded retry survives a controller restart (no in-memory counter).
279	type BoundaryPushStatus struct {
280		// Attempts is the number of boundary-push Jobs dispatched so far for this
281		// Project's run branch. The controller stops dispatching once Attempts
282		// reaches the in-controller cap (maxBoundaryPushAttempts) — bounded retry,
283		// no push-loop.
284		// +optional
285		Attempts int32 `json:"attempts,omitempty"`
286	
287		// LastAttemptTime is the timestamp of the most recently dispatched boundary
288		// push attempt. Drives the capped exponential requeue backoff.
289		// +optional
290		LastAttemptTime *metav1.Time `json:"lastAttemptTime,omitempty"`
291	
292		// LastError carries the most recent boundary-push failure reason (from the
293		// push-result envelope) for operator visibility. Cleared on success.
294		// +optional
295		LastError string `json:"lastError,omitempty"`
296	}
297	
298	// ProjectSpec defines the desired state of Project.
299	// +kubebuilder:validation:XValidation:rule="self.targetRepo.startsWith('http://') || self.targetRepo.startsWith('https://') || self.targetRepo.startsWith('git@')",message="targetRepo must be an http(s) or SSH (git@) URL; file:// is not a supported production transport (go-git's file:// transport requires a system git binary absent from production images)"
300	type ProjectSpec struct {
301		// TargetRepo is the URL of the repo this Project operates on. Supports
302		// http:// and https:// (pure-Go go-git transport, production default) and
303		// SSH (`git@host:owner/repo.git`, documented with host-key caveats).
304		// file:// is NOT supported: go-git's file:// transport requires a system
305		// git binary absent from production images.
306		// +kubebuilder:validation:MinLength=1
307		TargetRepo string `json:"targetRepo"`
308	
309		// OutcomePrompt is the human-authored outcome statement that TIDE turns
310		// into a phase brief + PLAN.md + tasks (Phase 5 BOOT-04). The planner
311		// subagent reads this verbatim from .spec; the v1.0 stub-subagent ignores
312		// it (small samples can leave it empty for the smoke-test path).
313		// Multi-line YAML literals (`|`) are the conventional shape.
314		// +optional
315		OutcomePrompt string `json:"outcomePrompt,omitempty"`
316	
317		// SecretRefs references K8s Secrets for credentials (AUTH-01 — Phase 3).
318		// +optional
319		SecretRefs SecretRefs `json:"secretRefs,omitempty"`
320	
321		// ModelSelection picks per-level model identifiers (Phase 2+).
322		// +optional
323		ModelSelection ModelSelection `json:"modelSelection,omitempty"`
324	
325		// Gates declares per-level human gate policy (Phase 4).
326		// +optional
327		Gates Gates `json:"gates,omitempty"`
328	
329		// ProviderSecretRef is the name of the K8s Secret carrying provider credentials (Phase 2+).
330		// +optional
331		ProviderSecretRef string `json:"providerSecretRef,omitempty"`
332	
333		// Providers lists per-provider configuration (rate limits, etc.) (Phase 2+).
334		// +optional
335		Providers []ProviderConfig `json:"providers,omitempty"`
336	
337		// Budget declares cost/token caps for this project (Phase 2+).
338		// +optional
339		Budget BudgetConfig `json:"budget,omitempty"`
340	
341		// PlanAdmission controls file-touch policy during plan validation (Phase 2+).
342		// +optional
343		PlanAdmission PlanAdmissionConfig `json:"planAdmission,omitempty"`
344	
345		// MaxAttemptsPerTask caps the number of dispatch attempts per Task before
346		// the Task is marked failed (Phase 2+, consumed by TaskReconciler in Plan 09).
347		// +kubebuilder:validation:Minimum=1
348		// +kubebuilder:validation:Maximum=10
349		// +optional
350		MaxAttemptsPerTask int32 `json:"maxAttemptsPerTask,omitempty"`
351	
352		// Subagent declares provider+model selection for planner/executor dispatch
353		// (Phase 3 D-C2). When empty, Helm-chart defaults apply.
354		// +optional
355		Subagent SubagentConfig `json:"subagent,omitempty"`
356	
357		// Git declares the per-Project target repo + creds for artifact push
358		// (Phase 3 D-B6). Required for any Project whose lifecycle reaches push;
359		// optional in v1.0 only for purely transient/test Projects.
360		// Pointer so omitempty fully elides the field when absent — value-type
361		// GitConfig{} would serialize as `git: {repoURL: ""}` and trip the
362		// RepoURL pattern validation on existing Phase 2 test fixtures.
363		// +optional
364		Git *GitConfig `json:"git,omitempty"`
365	}
366	
367	// Project Phase constants for Project.Status.Phase (Plan 10 — init Job + budget gate).
368	const (
369		// PhasePending is the initial phase before any reconcile has run.
370		PhasePending = "Pending"
371		// PhaseInitialized is set when the init Job completes successfully.
372		PhaseInitialized = "Initialized"
373		// PhaseInitFailed is set when the init Job exits non-zero.
374		PhaseInitFailed = "InitFailed"
375		// PhaseBudgetExceeded is set when the project's absolute cost cap is exceeded.
376		PhaseBudgetExceeded = "BudgetExceeded"
377		// PhaseRunning is set when dispatch is actively proceeding.
378		PhaseRunning = "Running"
379		// PhasePushLeaseFailed is set when a push Job rejects due to --force-with-lease
380		// mismatch (Phase 3 D-B6). Recovery: kubectl annotate project
381		// tideproject.k8s/bypass-push-lease=true (mirrors D-D4 bypass-budget pattern).
382		PhasePushLeaseFailed = "PushLeaseFailed"
383		// PhasePushLeakBlocked is set when a push Job exits with code 10 (gitleaks
384		// finding — envelope.reason=leak-detected). Distinct from PhasePushLeaseFailed
385		// (exit-11) so the project_controller switch can fire
386		// tide_secret_leak_blocked_total only on the leak-class outcome (Phase 4 D-W1).
387		// Recovery: operator inspects the diff and either drops the leaked secret
388		// artifact or rotates the leaked credential, then clears the phase.
389		PhasePushLeakBlocked = "PushLeakBlocked"
390		// PhaseComplete is the terminal success phase — set when all Milestones
391		// reach Succeeded and the final push Job lands (Phase 3 D-B2 #4).
392		PhaseComplete = "Complete"
393	)
394	
395	// ProjectStatus defines the observed state of Project.
396	// PERSIST-02 / Pitfall 4: NO aggregate schedule fields here.
397	type ProjectStatus struct {
398		// Phase is a high-level state label ("Pending", "Running", "Complete", "Failed").
399		// +optional
400		Phase string `json:"phase,omitempty"`
401	
402		// Conditions follow the standard K8s convention.
403		// +listType=map
404		// +listMapKey=type
405		// +optional
406		Conditions []metav1.Condition `json:"conditions,omitempty"`
407	
408		// Budget records running spend tallies (Phase 2+).
409		// PERSIST-02 / Pitfall 4: BudgetStatus is a tally object, not an aggregate schedule.
410		// +optional
411		Budget BudgetStatus `json:"budget,omitempty"`
412	
413		// Git records per-Project push state — branch name, last pushed SHA, and
414		// lease failure counter (Phase 3 D-B6). PERSIST-02 / Pitfall 4: this is a
415		// per-project tally object, NOT an aggregate schedule.
416		// +optional
417		Git GitStatus `json:"git,omitempty"`
418	
419		// BoundaryPush records the bounded auto-retry state of the project-level
420		// boundary push (debug defect #13b). NEVER gates Complete — surfaced via the
421		// non-terminal BoundaryPushed condition for observability + bounded recovery.
422		// +optional
423		BoundaryPush BoundaryPushStatus `json:"boundaryPush,omitempty"`
424	}
425	
426	// +kubebuilder:object:root=true
427	// +kubebuilder:subresource:status
428	// +kubebuilder:resource:scope=Namespaced
429	// +kubebuilder:printcolumn:name="Phase",type=string,JSONPath=".status.phase"
430	// +kubebuilder:printcolumn:name="Status",type=string,JSONPath=".status.conditions[?(@.type=='Ready')].status"
431	// +kubebuilder:printcolumn:name="Age",type=date,JSONPath=".metadata.creationTimestamp"
432	
433	// Project is the Schema for the projects API
434	type Project struct {
435		metav1.TypeMeta `json:",inline"`
436	
437		// metadata is a standard object metadata
438		// +optional
439		metav1.ObjectMeta `json:"metadata,omitzero"`
440	
441		// spec defines the desired state of Project
442		// +required
443		Spec ProjectSpec `json:"spec"`
444	
445		// status defines the observed state of Project
446		// +optional
447		Status ProjectStatus `json:"status,omitzero"`
448	}
449	
450	// +kubebuilder:object:root=true
451	
452	// ProjectList contains a list of Project
453	type ProjectList struct {
454		metav1.TypeMeta `json:",inline"`
455		metav1.ListMeta `json:"metadata,omitzero"`
456		Items           []Project `json:"items"`
457	}
458	
459	func init() {
460		SchemeBuilder.Register(&Project{}, &ProjectList{})
461	}
462

/workspace/worktrees/run-tide/run-dogfood-codex-runtime-1781739693/cmd/claude-subagent/main.go

/workspace/worktrees/run-tide/run-dogfood-codex-runtime-1781739693/cmd/credproxy/main.go
/workspace/worktrees/run-tide/run-dogfood-codex-runtime-1781739693/cmd/dashboard/api/events_sse.go
/workspace/worktrees/run-tide/run-dogfood-codex-runtime-1781739693/cmd/dashboard/api/events_sse_test.go

1	/*
2	Copyright 2026 TIDE Authors.
3	
4	Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
5	you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
6	You may obtain a copy of the License at
7	
8	    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
9	
10	Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
11	distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
12	WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
13	See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
14	limitations under the License.
15	*/
16	
17	// Env-variable helpers for Helm-driven configuration (Phase 3 plan 03-09).
18	//
19	// These helpers exist as testable units (cmd/manager/env_test.go) because
20	// the main() function itself is not unit-testable (it constructs the
21	// controller-runtime Manager and blocks on mgr.Start). Helpers cover the
22	// per-level model defaults (D-C4) and leader-election tuning (D-D1).
23	package main
24	
25	import (
26		"os"
27		"strconv"
28		"time"
29	
30		"github.com/jsquirrelz/tide/internal/controller"
31	)
32	
33	// envOrDefault returns the value of the env var named key, or fallback when
34	// the env var is unset or empty. Empty-string is treated as unset so a Helm
35	// value left at its zero default (e.g. `value: ""`) cleanly falls through to
36	// the binary's compile-time default rather than overriding it with "".
37	func envOrDefault(key, fallback string) string {
38		if v := os.Getenv(key); v != "" {
39			return v
40		}
41		return fallback
42	}
43	
44	// atoiOrDefault returns the integer value of the env var named key, or
45	// fallback when the env var is unset, empty, or not an integer.
46	// Non-integer values are tolerated (return fallback) so a stray non-numeric
47	// value in the Helm chart cannot crash-loop the controller.
48	func atoiOrDefault(key string, fallback int) int {
49		v := os.Getenv(key)
50		if v == "" {
51			return fallback
52		}
53		n, err := strconv.Atoi(v)
54		if err != nil {
55			return fallback
56		}
57		return n
58	}
59	
60	// resolvePerLevelModels returns the four-key map from level name
61	// ("milestone"|"phase"|"plan"|"task") to the model identifier used as the
62	// last-fallback in dispatch_helpers.ResolveProvider (D-C2).
63	//
64	// Defaults match D-C4 — phase brief lock-in:
65	//
66	//	milestone → claude-opus-4-7    (heavy planning, lowest fan-out)
67	//	phase     → claude-sonnet-4-6
68	//	plan      → claude-sonnet-4-6
69	//	task      → claude-haiku-4-5   (highest fan-out, cost-bounded)
70	//
71	// Override per-level via env vars (set by the manager Deployment from Helm
72	// values.yaml subagent.levels.{level}.model):
73	//
74	//	TIDE_DEFAULT_MODEL_MILESTONE
75	//	TIDE_DEFAULT_MODEL_PHASE
76	//	TIDE_DEFAULT_MODEL_PLAN
77	//	TIDE_DEFAULT_MODEL_TASK
78	func resolvePerLevelModels() map[string]string {
79		return map[string]string{
80			"milestone": envOrDefault("TIDE_DEFAULT_MODEL_MILESTONE", "claude-opus-4-7"),
81			"phase":     envOrDefault("TIDE_DEFAULT_MODEL_PHASE", "claude-sonnet-4-6"),
82			"plan":      envOrDefault("TIDE_DEFAULT_MODEL_PLAN", "claude-sonnet-4-6"),
83			"task":      envOrDefault("TIDE_DEFAULT_MODEL_TASK", "claude-haiku-4-5"),
84		}
85	}
86	
87	// tideHelmProviderDefaults builds the controller.ProviderDefaults struct
88	// (D-C2 last-fallback in ResolveProvider) from the Helm-chart subagent image
89	// + per-level model map. claudeImage is the default subagent image
90	// (CLAUDE_SUBAGENT_IMAGE env var); per-level models come from
91	// resolvePerLevelModels (TIDE_DEFAULT_MODEL_* env vars).
92	//
93	// Called once at Manager startup; the result is stamped onto each up-stack
94	// reconciler's HelmProviderDefaults field (MilestoneReconciler /
95	// PhaseReconciler / PlanReconciler).
96	func tideHelmProviderDefaults(claudeImage string) controller.ProviderDefaults {
97		return controller.ProviderDefaults{
98			Image:  claudeImage,
99			Models: resolvePerLevelModels(),
100		}
101	}
102	
103	// resolveLeaderElectionTiming returns the (lease, renew, retry) durations
104	// for controller-runtime's LeaderElection block (D-D1 / Phase 3 chaos-resume).
105	//
106	// Defaults pin to common controller-runtime production values
107	// (lease=15s, renew=10s, retry=2s) which give a single-pod restart ≤25s
108	// failover ceiling, with the invariant lease > renew > retry preserved.
109	// Override via env vars (set by manager Deployment from Helm values.yaml
110	// leaderElection.*):
111	//
112	//	TIDE_LEADER_LEASE_SECONDS  (default 15)
113	//	TIDE_LEADER_RENEW_SECONDS  (default 10)
114	//	TIDE_LEADER_RETRY_SECONDS  (default 2)
115	func resolveLeaderElectionTiming() (lease, renew, retry time.Duration) {
116		lease = time.Duration(atoiOrDefault("TIDE_LEADER_LEASE_SECONDS", 15)) * time.Second
117		renew = time.Duration(atoiOrDefault("TIDE_LEADER_RENEW_SECONDS", 10)) * time.Second
118		retry = time.Duration(atoiOrDefault("TIDE_LEADER_RETRY_SECONDS", 2)) * time.Second
119		return lease, renew, retry
120	}
121

1	/*
2	Copyright 2026 TIDE Authors.
3	
4	Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
5	you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
6	You may obtain a copy of the License at
7	
8	    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
9	
10	Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
11	distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
12	WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
13	See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
14	limitations under the License.
15	*/
16	
17	// Command claude-subagent — Phase 3 real-Claude swap of stub-subagent. Thin
18	// shim: load EnvelopeIn → anthropic.Run → write EnvelopeOut. Ignores the
19	// envelope's Dev struct (PATTERNS.md line 442 / D-F1).
20	package main
21	
22	import (
23		"context"
24		"encoding/json"
25		"flag"
26		"fmt"
27		"io"
28		"os"
29		"os/signal"
30		"path/filepath"
31		"syscall"
32		"time"
33	
34		"github.com/go-git/go-git/v5/plumbing"
35		"github.com/jsquirrelz/tide/internal/harness"
36		"github.com/jsquirrelz/tide/internal/subagent/anthropic"
37		pkgdispatch "github.com/jsquirrelz/tide/pkg/dispatch"
38	)
39	
40	// parsePricingOverridesFromEnv reads TIDE_PRICING_OVERRIDES_JSON and parses it.
41	// An empty or missing env var returns a nil map (no overrides). An invalid JSON
42	// value logs a loud stderr warning and returns nil (bad override must not fail
43	// the session — Plan 14-05 validates at manager startup; this is defense in depth).
44	func parsePricingOverridesFromEnv(stderr io.Writer) map[string]pkgdispatch.PriceOverride {
45		raw := os.Getenv("TIDE_PRICING_OVERRIDES_JSON")
46		if raw == "" {
47			return nil
48		}
49		overrides, err := pkgdispatch.ParsePricingOverrides(raw)
50		if err != nil {
51			fmt.Fprintf(stderr, "pricing: invalid TIDE_PRICING_OVERRIDES_JSON, ignoring overrides: %v\n", err)
52			return nil
53		}
54		return overrides
55	}
56	
57	type anthropicRunner interface {
58		Run(ctx context.Context, in pkgdispatch.EnvelopeIn) (pkgdispatch.EnvelopeOut, error)
59	}
60	
61	var newSubagent = func(
62		claudeBinary, workspaceRoot string,
63		pricingOverrides map[string]pkgdispatch.PriceOverride,
64	) anthropicRunner {
65		return anthropic.New(anthropic.Options{
66			ClaudeBinary:     claudeBinary,
67			WorkspaceRoot:    workspaceRoot,
68			PricingOverrides: pricingOverrides,
69		})
70	}
71	var ensureWorktreeFunc = harness.EnsureWorktree
72	var commitWorktreeFunc = func(worktreeDir, taskUID string) (plumbing.Hash, bool, error) {
73		return harness.CommitWorktree(worktreeDir, taskUID)
74	}
75	
76	func main() {
77		fs := flag.NewFlagSet("claude-subagent", flag.ExitOnError)
78		envelopePath := fs.String("envelope-path", "",
79			"path to EnvelopeIn JSON (default: $TIDE_ENVELOPE_PATH or /workspace/envelopes/$TIDE_TASK_UID/in.json)")
80		workspaceRoot := fs.String("workspace-root", "/workspace", "PVC mount root")
81		if err := fs.Parse(os.Args[1:]); err != nil {
82			os.Exit(2)
83		}
84		// Resolve envelope path: flag → $TIDE_ENVELOPE_PATH → per-task-uid default.
85		// This MUST match stub-subagent (cmd/stub-subagent/main.go) and the
86		// controller's write location, which is /workspace/envelopes/$TIDE_TASK_UID/in.json
87		// (the controller passes no --envelope-path arg, so it relies on this default).
88		// The previous hardcoded /workspace/envelopes/in.json default did not include
89		// the per-task-uid subdirectory, so the real claude planner/executor could never
90		// find its input envelope. Only surfaced in live real-Claude runs — CI exercises
91		// the stub, whose default already matched.
92		if *envelopePath == "" {
93			if p := os.Getenv("TIDE_ENVELOPE_PATH"); p != "" {
94				*envelopePath = p
95			} else {
96				*envelopePath = fmt.Sprintf("/workspace/envelopes/%s/in.json", os.Getenv("TIDE_TASK_UID"))
97			}
98		}
99		ctx, cancel := signal.NotifyContext(context.Background(), syscall.SIGTERM, syscall.SIGINT)
100		defer cancel()
101		os.Exit(run(ctx, *envelopePath, *workspaceRoot, os.Stdout, os.Stderr))
102	}
103	
104	// run is the testable entry point. Does NOT branch on env.Dev.TestMode.
105	//
106	//nolint:unparam // stdout is part of the shared subagent-binary (stdout, stderr) entry-point contract
107	func run(ctx context.Context, envelopePath, workspaceRoot string, stdout, stderr io.Writer) int {
108		outPath := filepath.Join(filepath.Dir(envelopePath), "out.json")
109		env, err := harness.ReadEnvelopeIn(envelopePath)
110		if err != nil {
111			return failOut(stderr, outPath, "", err, 2, "invalid-envelope")
112		}
113		if err := ensureWorktreeFunc(env, workspaceRoot, env.Branch); err != nil {
114			return failOut(stderr, outPath, env.TaskUID, err, 1, "worktree-setup-failed")
115		}
116		// D-02: read pricing overrides from TIDE_PRICING_OVERRIDES_JSON env.
117		// Invalid JSON logs a loud warning and falls back to compiled table (defense in depth).
118		pricingOverrides := parsePricingOverridesFromEnv(stderr)
119		out, runErr := newSubagent("claude", workspaceRoot, pricingOverrides).Run(ctx, env)
120		if runErr != nil {
121			fmt.Fprintf(stderr, "claude-subagent: %v\n", runErr)
122			out = failEnvelope(env.TaskUID, runErr, 1, "subagent-error")
123		}
124		if runErr == nil && env.Role == "executor" {
125			worktreeDir := filepath.Join(workspaceRoot, "worktrees", env.TaskUID)
126			hash, isEmpty, commitErr := commitWorktreeFunc(worktreeDir, env.TaskUID)
127			if commitErr != nil {
128				out = failEnvelope(env.TaskUID, commitErr, 1, "commit-failed")
129			} else if isEmpty {
130				out.ExitCode = 1
131				out.Result = "empty-diff"
132				out.Reason = "executor produced no changes in worktree"
133			} else {
134				out.Git = &pkgdispatch.GitOutput{HeadSHA: hash.String()}
135			}
136		}
137		if err := writeEnvelope(outPath, out); err != nil {
138			fmt.Fprintf(stderr, "claude-subagent: write out.json: %v\n", err)
139			return 2
140		}
141		return out.ExitCode
142	}
143	
144	func failEnvelope(taskUID string, err error, exitCode int, result string) pkgdispatch.EnvelopeOut {
145		return pkgdispatch.EnvelopeOut{
146			APIVersion: pkgdispatch.APIVersionV1Alpha1, Kind: pkgdispatch.KindTaskEnvelopeOut,
147			TaskUID: taskUID, ExitCode: exitCode, Result: result, Reason: err.Error(),
148			CompletedAt: time.Now().UTC(),
149		}
150	}
151	
152	func failOut(stderr io.Writer, outPath, taskUID string, err error, exitCode int, result string) int {
153		fmt.Fprintf(stderr, "claude-subagent: %v\n", err)
154		_ = writeEnvelope(outPath, failEnvelope(taskUID, err, exitCode, result))
155		return exitCode
156	}
157	
158	func writeEnvelope(path string, out pkgdispatch.EnvelopeOut) error {
159		// Write the full envelope to the PVC (audit artifact + reporter Job input).
160		// This is UNCHANGED — out.json on the PVC carries ChildCRDs + verbose Result.
161		if err := harness.WriteEnvelopeOut(path, out); err != nil {
162			return err
163		}
164		// Write only the tiny TerminationStub to the termination message path.
165		// The stub carries ExitCode/Reason/Usage/HeadSHA and stays <4KB regardless
166		// of ChildCRDs or Result size (#11 root cause fix; T-09-03 mitigation).
167		stub := pkgdispatch.NewTerminationStub(out)
168		data, _ := json.Marshal(stub)
169		tp := os.Getenv("TIDE_TERMINATION_MESSAGE_PATH")
170		if tp == "" {
171			tp = "/dev/termination-log"
172		}
173		_ = os.WriteFile(tp, data, 0o644)
174		return nil
175	}
176

# TIDE controller chart values (Phase 1 surface — D-E1).
#
# This file is the canonical, hand-maintained values.yaml for charts/tide/. The
# `make helm-controller` target generates charts/tide/ via helmify, then copies
# THIS file over the helmify-emitted charts/tide/values.yaml. Helmify cannot
# infer (a) ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/* image registry, (b) the Phase 1 tunables
# (plannerConcurrency, executorConcurrency, maxConcurrentReconciles), or (c)
# leaderElection block — those are project-level decisions that live here,
# version-controlled separately from helmify's regenerated output.
#
# Phase 5 will extend this with dashboard.enabled, prometheus.serviceMonitor.enabled,
# and additional OSS-distribution flags (D-E2). The structure below — helmify-emitted
# controllerManager.* keys plus hand-augmented top-level Phase 1 tunables — is the
# Phase 5-compatible foundation.

# Image refs (D-A2: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/* registry).
controllerManager:
  manager:
    args:
    - --metrics-bind-address=:8443
    - --config=/etc/tide/config.yaml
    - --watch-namespace=$(WATCH_NAMESPACE)
    - --leader-elect
    - --webhook-cert-path=/tmp/k8s-webhook-server/serving-certs
    containerSecurityContext:
      allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
      capabilities:
        drop:
        - ALL
      readOnlyRootFilesystem: true
    env:
      # watchNamespace = "" means watch ALL namespaces (cluster-scoped install posture,
      # AUTH-02). Set this to a specific namespace for per-namespace install — the
      # cmd/manager/main.go --watch-namespace flag wires it through to the Manager's
      # cache.ByObject namespace filter.
      watchNamespace: ""
    image:
      repository: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-controller
      tag: ""
      pullPolicy: IfNotPresent
    podAnnotations: {}
    resources:
      limits:
        cpu: 500m
        memory: 128Mi
      requests:
        cpu: 10m
        memory: 64Mi
  podSecurityContext:
    runAsNonRoot: true
    seccompProfile:
      type: RuntimeDefault
  replicas: 1
  serviceAccount:
    annotations: {}
kubernetesClusterDomain: cluster.local
metricsService:
  ports:
  - name: https
    port: 8443
    protocol: TCP
    targetPort: 8443
  type: ClusterIP
webhookService:
  ports:
  - port: 443
    protocol: TCP
    targetPort: 9443
  type: ClusterIP

# Phase 1 runtime tunables (D-E1) — rendered into the tide-config ConfigMap which is
# mounted at /etc/tide/config.yaml and parsed by internal/config.Load (CTRL-04 / Plan 04).
#
# Two distinct parallelism budgets per Pitfall 6: planning fans out wide (Milestone/
# Phase/Plan reconciles), execution fans out narrow (Wave/Task reconciles). The
# crosspool analyzer (cmd/tide-lint) prevents the two budgets from being unified at
# compile time; this values surface keeps them separately tunable at deploy time.
plannerConcurrency: 16
executorConcurrency: 4

# Per-Kind controller-runtime MaxConcurrentReconciles. Values match the Plan 04
# defaults — see internal/config/defaults.go. Project/Milestone are singletons-per-
# parent in the common case (1 is enough); Wave/Task fan out wider.
maxConcurrentReconciles:
  project: 1
  milestone: 1
  phase: 2
  plan: 4
  wave: 8
  task: 16

# CTRL-03 leader election. Enabled by default; the controller's
# --leader-elect arg is appended in controllerManager.manager.args above.
#
# Phase 3 plan 03-09 (D-D1 chaos-resume tuning): leaseDurationSeconds,
# renewDeadlineSeconds, and retryPeriodSeconds flow through to
# controller-runtime via TIDE_LEADER_{LEASE,RENEW,RETRY}_SECONDS env vars on
# the manager Deployment. Defaults give a single-pod restart ≤25s failover
# ceiling. Invariant: lease > renew > retry (controller-runtime validates).
leaderElection:

content.content:
#   --set subagent.defaults.image=ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-stub-subagent:<tag>
# Bare refs (no ":<tag>" or "@sha256:" suffix) have ":<appVersion>" appended by the
# chart template. Tag- or digest-qualified values pass through verbatim.
# Digest pinning is recommended for production reproducibility:
#   --set subagent.defaults.image=ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-claude-subagent@sha256:<digest>
#
# Per-level model defaults (D-C4) match the phase-brief lock-in:
#   milestone → claude-opus-4-7    (heavy planning, lowest fan-out)
#   phase     → claude-sonnet-4-6
#   plan      → claude-sonnet-4-6
#   task      → claude-haiku-4-5   (highest fan-out, cost-bounded)
#
# Per-level models flow through the manager Deployment env block as
# TIDE_DEFAULT_MODEL_{MILESTONE,PHASE,PLAN,TASK}.
subagent:
  defaults:
    image: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-claude-subagent
    model: claude-sonnet-4-6
  levels:
    milestone:
      model: claude-opus-4-7
    phase:
      model: claude-sonnet-4-6
    plan:
      model: claude-sonnet-4-6
    task:
      model: claude-haiku-4-5

# Phase 3 plan 03-09 (D-B3): optional cluster-wide gitleaks override.
# Empty (default) → push Job uses the compiled-in gitleaks v8 ruleset.
# Per-Project overrides are still supported via Project.Spec.git.leaksConfigRef
# (D-B3 — ConfigMap mounted into push Job at /etc/tide/gitleaks-config.toml).
# This top-level key is the cluster-wide fallback (v1.0 deferred; reserved for v1.x).
gitleaks:
  configMapName: ""

# HMAC signing-secret name (D-C3 / Blocker #1 fix).
# The signing-secret.yaml Helm template creates a Secret with this name on first install
# using Helm's `lookup` + `if not` guard (idempotent). resource-policy: keep annotation
# preserves the Secret across helm upgrade/uninstall so in-flight tokens stay valid
# (T-02-12-02 / T-02-12-03 mitigations).
# The Secret's data key is TIDE_SIGNING_KEY (env-friendly) so envFrom: [{secretRef: ...}]
# auto-populates the TIDE_SIGNING_KEY env var on the Manager Deployment and credproxy sidecar.
signingKey:
  secretName: tide-signing-key

# Budget defaults (D-D2 / FAIL-04). Helm-driven hard caps applied to all Projects that
# do not specify per-Project caps in Project.Spec.Budget.
budget:
  # D-05: flat per-dispatch reservation estimate in cents pre-charged against the budget
  # cap at Job creation; bounds in-flight overshoot to estimate error instead of wave width;
  # 0 disables pre-charge (cap still enforced at dispatch via the BudgetBlocked condition).
  # Passed via --budget-reserve-per-dispatch-cents.
  reservePerDispatchCents: 100
  defaults:
    absoluteCapCents: 10000      # $100.00 default absolute cap
    rollingWindowCapCents: 5000  # $50.00 / 15-min rolling window default

# Phase 4 additions (Plan 04-14 — D-X3 / D-D2 / D-O6 / D-O3).
#
# All three blocks are ADDITIVE. The Phase 1 surface above is unchanged
# (CLAUDE.md "chart is FIXED contract — binary catches up to chart, never
# reverse" anti-pattern). Phase 4 chart additions land here without touching
# any pre-Phase-4 keys.

# Read-only dashboard backend (Phase 4 D-D2). Ships as a separate
# Deployment + Service + ServiceAccount + ClusterRole + ClusterRoleBinding
# under templates/dashboard-{deployment,service,rbac}.yaml. The dashboard
# ClusterRole is enforced read-only at chart-render time via
# `make helm-rbac-assert` (T-04-D2 mitigation).
#
# Default ON because the dashboard is one of the four Phase 4 deliverables;
# operators who want the controller-only install set
# `--set dashboard.enabled=false`.
dashboard:
  enabled: true
  image:
    repository: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-dashboard
    tag: ""             # default to .Chart.AppVersion
    pullPolicy: IfNotPresent
  replicas: 1
  resources:
    requests:
      cpu: 50m
      memory: 64Mi
    limits:
      cpu: 200m
      memory: 256Mi
  service:
    type: ClusterIP
    port: 80
    targetPort: 8080

# Prometheus ServiceMonitor (Phase 4 D-O6).
#
# DEFAULT OFF per CLAUDE.md anti-pattern:
#   "Default the chart's ServiceMonitor to prometheus.enabled=false to avoid
#    CRD-not-found on plain clusters."
#
# Enable explicitly via `--set prometheus.serviceMonitor.enabled=true` once
# the prometheus-operator CRDs (monitoring.coreos.com/v1) are installed.
prometheus:
  # PromQL-proxy endpoint (Phase 4 telemetry surface -- milestone-01 Q1 lock-in).
  #
  # Q1 DECISION: PROXY, not a second browser-side datasource. This is a
  # SERVER-SIDE value. When non-empty it is injected as the PROM_ENDPOINT env
  # var into the dashboard container (templates/dashboard-deployment.yaml, landed
  # by plan-02), and the dashboard chi server proxies PromQL via
  # GET /api/v1/query and GET /api/v1/query_range. The browser therefore never
  # learns a second network target: single origin, no CORS, one auth surface,
  # and the Prometheus URL stays confined to the dashboard process (matters when
  # Prometheus is ClusterIP-only and unreachable from outside the cluster).
  #
  # Empty (default) => graceful degradation: the proxy returns HTTP 200 with the
  # body {"status":"unavailable"} and the React Telemetry tab falls back to

tool_use_result.stdout:
#   --set subagent.defaults.image=ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-stub-subagent:<tag>
# Bare refs (no ":<tag>" or "@sha256:" suffix) have ":<appVersion>" appended by the
# chart template. Tag- or digest-qualified values pass through verbatim.
# Digest pinning is recommended for production reproducibility:
#   --set subagent.defaults.image=ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-claude-subagent@sha256:<digest>
#
# Per-level model defaults (D-C4) match the phase-brief lock-in:
#   milestone → claude-opus-4-7    (heavy planning, lowest fan-out)
#   phase     → claude-sonnet-4-6
#   plan      → claude-sonnet-4-6
#   task      → claude-haiku-4-5   (highest fan-out, cost-bounded)
#
# Per-level models flow through the manager Deployment env block as
# TIDE_DEFAULT_MODEL_{MILESTONE,PHASE,PLAN,TASK}.
subagent:
  defaults:
    image: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-claude-subagent
    model: claude-sonnet-4-6
  levels:
    milestone:
      model: claude-opus-4-7
    phase:
      model: claude-sonnet-4-6
    plan:
      model: claude-sonnet-4-6
    task:
      model: claude-haiku-4-5

# Phase 3 plan 03-09 (D-B3): optional cluster-wide gitleaks override.
# Empty (default) → push Job uses the compiled-in gitleaks v8 ruleset.
# Per-Project overrides are still supported via Project.Spec.git.leaksConfigRef
# (D-B3 — ConfigMap mounted into push Job at /etc/tide/gitleaks-config.toml).
# This top-level key is the cluster-wide fallback (v1.0 deferred; reserved for v1.x).
gitleaks:
  configMapName: ""

# HMAC signing-secret name (D-C3 / Blocker #1 fix).
# The signing-secret.yaml Helm template creates a Secret with this name on first install
# using Helm's `lookup` + `if not` guard (idempotent). resource-policy: keep annotation
# preserves the Secret across helm upgrade/uninstall so in-flight tokens stay valid
# (T-02-12-02 / T-02-12-03 mitigations).
# The Secret's data key is TIDE_SIGNING_KEY (env-friendly) so envFrom: [{secretRef: ...}]
# auto-populates the TIDE_SIGNING_KEY env var on the Manager Deployment and credproxy sidecar.
signingKey:
  secretName: tide-signing-key

# Budget defaults (D-D2 / FAIL-04). Helm-driven hard caps applied to all Projects that
# do not specify per-Project caps in Project.Spec.Budget.
budget:
  # D-05: flat per-dispatch reservation estimate in cents pre-charged against the budget
  # cap at Job creation; bounds in-flight overshoot to estimate error instead of wave width;
  # 0 disables pre-charge (cap still enforced at dispatch via the BudgetBlocked condition).
  # Passed via --budget-reserve-per-dispatch-cents.
  reservePerDispatchCents: 100
  defaults:
    absoluteCapCents: 10000      # $100.00 default absolute cap
    rollingWindowCapCents: 5000  # $50.00 / 15-min rolling window default

# Phase 4 additions (Plan 04-14 — D-X3 / D-D2 / D-O6 / D-O3).
#
# All three blocks are ADDITIVE. The Phase 1 surface above is unchanged
# (CLAUDE.md "chart is FIXED contract — binary catches up to chart, never
# reverse" anti-pattern). Phase 4 chart additions land here without touching
# any pre-Phase-4 keys.

# Read-only dashboard backend (Phase 4 D-D2). Ships as a separate
# Deployment + Service + ServiceAccount + ClusterRole + ClusterRoleBinding
# under templates/dashboard-{deployment,service,rbac}.yaml. The dashboard
# ClusterRole is enforced read-only at chart-render time via
# `make helm-rbac-assert` (T-04-D2 mitigation).
#
# Default ON because the dashboard is one of the four Phase 4 deliverables;
# operators who want the controller-only install set
# `--set dashboard.enabled=false`.
dashboard:
  enabled: true
  image:
    repository: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-dashboard
    tag: ""             # default to .Chart.AppVersion
    pullPolicy: IfNotPresent
  replicas: 1
  resources:
    requests:
      cpu: 50m
      memory: 64Mi
    limits:
      cpu: 200m
      memory: 256Mi
  service:
    type: ClusterIP
    port: 80
    targetPort: 8080

# Prometheus ServiceMonitor (Phase 4 D-O6).
#
# DEFAULT OFF per CLAUDE.md anti-pattern:
#   "Default the chart's ServiceMonitor to prometheus.enabled=false to avoid
#    CRD-not-found on plain clusters."
#
# Enable explicitly via `--set prometheus.serviceMonitor.enabled=true` once
# the prometheus-operator CRDs (monitoring.coreos.com/v1) are installed.
prometheus:
  # PromQL-proxy endpoint (Phase 4 telemetry surface -- milestone-01 Q1 lock-in).
  #
  # Q1 DECISION: PROXY, not a second browser-side datasource. This is a
  # SERVER-SIDE value. When non-empty it is injected as the PROM_ENDPOINT env
  # var into the dashboard container (templates/dashboard-deployment.yaml, landed
  # by plan-02), and the dashboard chi server proxies PromQL via
  # GET /api/v1/query and GET /api/v1/query_range. The browser therefore never
  # learns a second network target: single origin, no CORS, one auth surface,
  # and the Prometheus URL stays confined to the dashboard process (matters when
  # Prometheus is ClusterIP-only and unreachable from outside the cluster).
  #
  # Empty (default) => graceful degradation: the proxy returns HTTP 200 with the
  # body {"status":"unavailable"} and the React Telemetry tab falls back to

1	apiVersion: apps/v1
2	kind: Deployment
3	metadata:
4	  name: {{ include "tide.fullname" . }}-controller-manager
5	  labels:
6	    control-plane: controller-manager
7	  {{- include "tide.labels" . | nindent 4 }}
8	spec:
9	  replicas: {{ .Values.controllerManager.replicas }}
10	  selector:
11	    matchLabels:
12	      app.kubernetes.io/name: tide
13	      control-plane: controller-manager
14	    {{- include "tide.selectorLabels" . | nindent 6 }}
15	  template:
16	    metadata:
17	      labels:
18	        app.kubernetes.io/name: tide
19	        control-plane: controller-manager
20	      {{- include "tide.selectorLabels" . | nindent 8 }}
21	      annotations:
22	        kubectl.kubernetes.io/default-container: manager
23	        {{- with .Values.controllerManager.manager.podAnnotations }}
24	        {{- toYaml . | nindent 8 }}
25	        {{- end }}
26	    spec:
27	      containers:
28	      - args:
29	          {{- toYaml .Values.controllerManager.manager.args | nindent 10 }}
30	          - --credproxy-image={{ .Values.images.credProxy.repository }}:{{ .Values.images.credProxy.tag | default .Chart.AppVersion }}
31	          - --default-file-touch-mode={{ .Values.planAdmission.fileTouchMode | default "warn" }}
32	          - --rate-limit-default-rpm={{ .Values.rateLimits.defaults.requestsPerMinute | default 60 }}
33	          - --rate-limit-default-burst={{ .Values.rateLimits.defaults.burst | default 10 }}
34	          - --budget-reserve-per-dispatch-cents={{ .Values.budget.reservePerDispatchCents | default 100 }}
35	          {{- if .Values.pricing.overrides }}
36	          - --pricing-overrides-json={{ .Values.pricing.overrides | toJson }}
37	          {{- end }}
38	          # phase2-args-injected
39	        command:
40	        - /manager
41	        env:
42	        - name: WATCH_NAMESPACE
43	          value: {{ quote .Values.controllerManager.manager.env.watchNamespace }}
44	        - name: KUBERNETES_CLUSTER_DOMAIN
45	          value: {{ quote .Values.kubernetesClusterDomain }}
46	        - name: TIDE_PUSH_IMAGE
47	          value: "{{ .Values.images.tidePush.repository }}:{{ .Values.images.tidePush.tag | default .Chart.AppVersion }}"
48	        # Phase 13 D-01: subagent image default channel.
49	        # Resolution chain (highest priority wins):
50	        #   Levels.<level>.Image (Project CRD per-level override)
51	        #   → Spec.Subagent.Image (Project CRD per-project default)
52	        #   → CLAUDE_SUBAGENT_IMAGE (this env — Helm chart default)
53	        #
54	        # The startup flag injecting the stub image has been removed (Phase 13
55	        # D-01 deliberate fixed-contract exception). This env is now the sole
56	        # default tier.
57	        #
58	        # Stub opt-in for tests/CI:
59	        #   --set subagent.defaults.image=ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-stub-subagent:<tag>
60	        # Tag- or digest-qualified values (containing ":" after last "/" or "@sha256:")
61	        # pass through verbatim. Bare refs get ":<appVersion>" appended automatically.
62	        # Digest pinning passes through unchanged — recommended for production.
63	        - name: CLAUDE_SUBAGENT_IMAGE
64	          {{- $img := required "subagent.defaults.image must be a non-empty image ref (e.g. ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-stub-subagent:latest)" .Values.subagent.defaults.image }}
65	          {{- if or (regexMatch ":[^/]+$" $img) (contains "@" $img) }}
66	          value: {{ $img | quote }}
67	          {{- else }}
68	          value: {{ printf "%s:%s" $img .Chart.AppVersion | quote }}
69	          {{- end }}
70	        - name: TIDE_DEFAULT_MODEL_MILESTONE
71	          value: "{{ .Values.subagent.levels.milestone.model | default "claude-opus-4-7" }}"
72	        - name: TIDE_DEFAULT_MODEL_PHASE
73	          value: "{{ .Values.subagent.levels.phase.model | default "claude-sonnet-4-6" }}"
74	        - name: TIDE_DEFAULT_MODEL_PLAN
75	          value: "{{ .Values.subagent.levels.plan.model | default "claude-sonnet-4-6" }}"
76	        - name: TIDE_DEFAULT_MODEL_TASK
77	          value: "{{ .Values.subagent.levels.task.model | default "claude-haiku-4-5" }}"
78	        - name: TIDE_LEADER_LEASE_SECONDS
79	          value: "{{ .Values.leaderElection.leaseDurationSeconds | default 15 }}"
80	        - name: TIDE_LEADER_RENEW_SECONDS
81	          value: "{{ .Values.leaderElection.renewDeadlineSeconds | default 10 }}"
82	        - name: TIDE_LEADER_RETRY_SECONDS
83	          value: "{{ .Values.leaderElection.retryPeriodSeconds | default 2 }}"
84	        # phase3-env-injected
85	        # Phase 4 plan 04-14 (D-O3): OTel env vars read by internal/otelinit.
86	        # Empty OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT → no-op TracerProvider (zero
87	        # overhead, default posture for plain clusters). Sampler is env-driven
88	        # to honor Pitfall 24 mitigation (no WithSampler in code).
89	        - name: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT
90	          value: {{ quote .Values.otel.exporter.endpoint }}
91	        - name: OTEL_TRACES_SAMPLER
92	          value: {{ quote .Values.otel.tracesSampler }}
93	        - name: OTEL_TRACES_SAMPLER_ARG
94	          value: {{ quote .Values.otel.tracesSamplerArg }}
95	        - name: OTEL_SERVICE_NAME
96	          value: {{ quote .Values.otel.serviceName }}
97	        # phase4-env-injected
98	        - name: TIDE_REPORTER_IMAGE
99	          value: "{{ .Values.images.tideReporter.repository }}:{{ .Values.images.tideReporter.tag | default .Chart.AppVersion }}"
100	        # phase9-reporter-env-injected
101	
102	        envFrom:
103	        - secretRef:
104	            name: {{ .Values.signingKey.secretName | default "tide-signing-key" }}
105	        image: {{ .Values.controllerManager.manager.image.repository }}:{{ .Values.controllerManager.manager.image.tag
106	          | default .Chart.AppVersion }}
107	        livenessProbe:
108	          httpGet:
109	            path: /healthz
110	            port: 8081
111	          initialDelaySeconds: 15
112	          periodSeconds: 20
113	        name: manager
114	        ports:
115	        - containerPort: 8080
116	          name: metrics
117	          protocol: TCP
118	        - containerPort: 8081
119	          name: health
120	          protocol: TCP
121	        - containerPort: 9443
122	          name: webhook-server
123	          protocol: TCP
124	        readinessProbe:
125	          httpGet:
126	            path: /readyz
127	            port: 8081
128	          initialDelaySeconds: 5
129	          periodSeconds: 10
130	        resources: {{- toYaml .Values.controllerManager.manager.resources | nindent 10
131	          }}
132	        securityContext: {{- toYaml .Values.controllerManager.manager.containerSecurityContext
133	          | nindent 10 }}
134	        volumeMounts:
135	        - mountPath: /etc/tide
136	          name: tide-config
137	          readOnly: true
138	        - mountPath: /tmp/k8s-webhook-server/serving-certs
139	          name: webhook-certs
140	          readOnly: true
141	        - mountPath: /workspaces
142	          name: tide-projects
143	          # phase2-vmount-injected
144	      securityContext: {{- toYaml .Values.controllerManager.podSecurityContext | nindent
145	        8 }}
146	      serviceAccountName: {{ include "tide.fullname" . }}-controller-manager
147	      terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 10
148	      volumes:
149	      - configMap:
150	          name: tide-config
151	          optional: true
152	        name: tide-config
153	      - name: webhook-certs
154	        secret:
155	          secretName: webhook-server-cert
156	      - name: tide-projects
157	        persistentVolumeClaim:
158	          claimName: {{ .Values.workspaces.pvc.name | default "tide-projects" }}
159	      # phase2-volume-injected

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: {{ include "tide.fullname" . }}-controller-manager
  labels:
    control-plane: controller-manager
  {{- include "tide.labels" . | nindent 4 }}
spec:
  replicas: {{ .Values.controllerManager.replicas }}
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/name: tide
      control-plane: controller-manager
    {{- include "tide.selectorLabels" . | nindent 6 }}
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/name: tide
        control-plane: controller-manager
      {{- include "tide.selectorLabels" . | nindent 8 }}
      annotations:
        kubectl.kubernetes.io/default-container: manager
        {{- with .Values.controllerManager.manager.podAnnotations }}
        {{- toYaml . | nindent 8 }}
        {{- end }}
    spec:
      containers:
      - args:
          {{- toYaml .Values.controllerManager.manager.args | nindent 10 }}
          - --credproxy-image={{ .Values.images.credProxy.repository }}:{{ .Values.images.credProxy.tag | default .Chart.AppVersion }}
          - --default-file-touch-mode={{ .Values.planAdmission.fileTouchMode | default "warn" }}
          - --rate-limit-default-rpm={{ .Values.rateLimits.defaults.requestsPerMinute | default 60 }}
          - --rate-limit-default-burst={{ .Values.rateLimits.defaults.burst | default 10 }}
          - --budget-reserve-per-dispatch-cents={{ .Values.budget.reservePerDispatchCents | default 100 }}
          {{- if .Values.pricing.overrides }}
          - --pricing-overrides-json={{ .Values.pricing.overrides | toJson }}
          {{- end }}
          # phase2-args-injected
        command:
        - /manager
        env:
        - name: WATCH_NAMESPACE
          value: {{ quote .Values.controllerManager.manager.env.watchNamespace }}
        - name: KUBERNETES_CLUSTER_DOMAIN
          value: {{ quote .Values.kubernetesClusterDomain }}
        - name: TIDE_PUSH_IMAGE
          value: "{{ .Values.images.tidePush.repository }}:{{ .Values.images.tidePush.tag | default .Chart.AppVersion }}"
        # Phase 13 D-01: subagent image default channel.
        # Resolution chain (highest priority wins):
        #   Levels.<level>.Image (Project CRD per-level override)
        #   → Spec.Subagent.Image (Project CRD per-project default)
        #   → CLAUDE_SUBAGENT_IMAGE (this env — Helm chart default)
        #
        # The startup flag injecting the stub image has been removed (Phase 13
        # D-01 deliberate fixed-contract exception). This env is now the sole
        # default tier.
        #
        # Stub opt-in for tests/CI:
        #   --set subagent.defaults.image=ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-stub-subagent:<tag>
        # Tag- or digest-qualified values (containing ":" after last "/" or "@sha256:")
        # pass through verbatim. Bare refs get ":<appVersion>" appended automatically.
        # Digest pinning passes through unchanged — recommended for production.
        - name: CLAUDE_SUBAGENT_IMAGE
          {{- $img := required "subagent.defaults.image must be a non-empty image ref (e.g. ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-stub-subagent:latest)" .Values.subagent.defaults.image }}
          {{- if or (regexMatch ":[^/]+$" $img) (contains "@" $img) }}
          value: {{ $img | quote }}
          {{- else }}
          value: {{ printf "%s:%s" $img .Chart.AppVersion | quote }}
          {{- end }}
        - name: TIDE_DEFAULT_MODEL_MILESTONE
          value: "{{ .Values.subagent.levels.milestone.model | default "claude-opus-4-7" }}"
        - name: TIDE_DEFAULT_MODEL_PHASE
          value: "{{ .Values.subagent.levels.phase.model | default "claude-sonnet-4-6" }}"
        - name: TIDE_DEFAULT_MODEL_PLAN
          value: "{{ .Values.subagent.levels.plan.model | default "claude-sonnet-4-6" }}"
        - name: TIDE_DEFAULT_MODEL_TASK
          value: "{{ .Values.subagent.levels.task.model | default "claude-haiku-4-5" }}"
        - name: TIDE_LEADER_LEASE_SECONDS
          value: "{{ .Values.leaderElection.leaseDurationSeconds | default 15 }}"
        - name: TIDE_LEADER_RENEW_SECONDS
          value: "{{ .Values.leaderElection.renewDeadlineSeconds | default 10 }}"
        - name: TIDE_LEADER_RETRY_SECONDS
          value: "{{ .Values.leaderElection.retryPeriodSeconds | default 2 }}"
        # phase3-env-injected
        # Phase 4 plan 04-14 (D-O3): OTel env vars read by internal/otelinit.
        # Empty OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT → no-op TracerProvider (zero
        # overhead, default posture for plain clusters). Sampler is env-driven
        # to honor Pitfall 24 mitigation (no WithSampler in code).
        - name: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT
          value: {{ quote .Values.otel.exporter.endpoint }}
        - name: OTEL_TRACES_SAMPLER
          value: {{ quote .Values.otel.tracesSampler }}
        - name: OTEL_TRACES_SAMPLER_ARG
          value: {{ quote .Values.otel.tracesSamplerArg }}
        - name: OTEL_SERVICE_NAME
          value: {{ quote .Values.otel.serviceName }}
        # phase4-env-injected
        - name: TIDE_REPORTER_IMAGE
          value: "{{ .Values.images.tideReporter.repository }}:{{ .Values.images.tideReporter.tag | default .Chart.AppVersion }}"
        # phase9-reporter-env-injected

        envFrom:
        - secretRef:
            name: {{ .Values.signingKey.secretName | default "tide-signing-key" }}
        image: {{ .Values.controllerManager.manager.image.repository }}:{{ .Values.controllerManager.manager.image.tag
          | default .Chart.AppVersion }}
        livenessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /healthz
            port: 8081
          initialDelaySeconds: 15
          periodSeconds: 20
        name: manager
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8080
          name: metrics
          protocol: TCP
        - containerPort: 8081
          name: health
          protocol: TCP
        - containerPort: 9443
          name: webhook-server
          protocol: TCP
        readinessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /readyz
            port: 8081
          initialDelaySeconds: 5
          periodSeconds: 10
        resources: {{- toYaml .Values.controllerManager.manager.resources | nindent 10
          }}
        securityContext: {{- toYaml .Values.controllerManager.manager.containerSecurityContext
          | nindent 10 }}
        volumeMounts:
        - mountPath: /etc/tide
          name: tide-config
          readOnly: true
        - mountPath: /tmp/k8s-webhook-server/serving-certs
          name: webhook-certs
          readOnly: true
        - mountPath: /workspaces
          name: tide-projects
          # phase2-vmount-injected
      securityContext: {{- toYaml .Values.controllerManager.podSecurityContext | nindent
        8 }}
      serviceAccountName: {{ include "tide.fullname" . }}-controller-manager
      terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 10
      volumes:
      - configMap:
          name: tide-config
          optional: true
        name: tide-config
      - name: webhook-certs
        secret:
          secretName: webhook-server-cert
      - name: tide-projects
        persistentVolumeClaim:
          claimName: {{ .Values.workspaces.pvc.name | default "tide-projects" }}
      # phase2-volume-injected

1	# examples/projects/medium/project.yaml — Phase 5 D-B2 medium sample (~$5 real Claude).
2	#
3	# This sample lets operators run TIDE against real Claude (Haiku 4.5, cost-disciplined
4	# per D-B1) with a hard $5 cap — no external public repo dependency (D-B3). The
5	# targetRepo is an in-cluster http:// git remote served by the tide-git-http-server
6	# Deployment (git http-backend CGI + nginx), backed by the demo-remote-pvc populated
7	# by the demo-remote-init Job. All git transport is pure-Go (go-git HTTP) — no git
8	# binary in core images required.
9	#
10	# APPLY ORDER (do NOT apply this file alone — see README.md):
11	#   1. namespace.yaml
12	#   2. demo-remote-pvc.yaml
13	#   3. per-namespace-resources.yaml      (tide-projects PVC + tide-subagent SA)
14	#   4. Mirror tide-signing-key Secret from tide-system (see per-namespace-resources.yaml)
15	#   5. demo-remote-init-job.yaml         (init Job populates the bare repo on demo-remote-pvc)
16	#      kubectl wait --for=condition=Complete job/demo-remote-init \
17	#        -n tide-sample-medium --timeout=2m
18	#   6. git-http-server-deployment.yaml   (after init Job completes — RWO PVC ordering)
19	#      kubectl wait --for=condition=Available deployment/git-http-server \
20	#        -n tide-sample-medium --timeout=2m
21	#   7. kubectl create secret generic tide-secrets \
22	#        --from-literal=ANTHROPIC_API_KEY="$ANTHROPIC_API_KEY" \
23	#        --from-literal=GIT_PAT="" \   # empty — anonymous in-cluster http:// push
24	#        -n tide-sample-medium
25	#   8. project.yaml                      (THIS file)
26	#   9. kubectl wait --for=jsonpath='{.status.phase}'=Complete \
27	#        project/medium-project -n tide-sample-medium --timeout=30m
28	#
29	# Schema notes:
30	#   - ProjectSpec.OutcomePrompt is a first-class Spec field read by the planner subagent.
31	#   - targetRepo uses http:// in-cluster URL (file:// rejected by CEL validator in Phase 8).
32	#   - GIT_PAT can be empty — tide-push push mode GIT_PAT guard is scheme-conditional
33	#     (Phase 8 08-05 Task 3): empty PAT is accepted for anonymous http:// remotes.
34	---
35	apiVersion: tideproject.k8s/v1alpha1
36	kind: Project
37	metadata:
38	  name: medium-project
39	  namespace: tide-sample-medium
40	  labels:
41	    app.kubernetes.io/name: tide
42	    app.kubernetes.io/managed-by: tide-sample
43	    tideproject.k8s/sample: medium
44	spec:
45	  # In-cluster anonymous git remote served by git-http-server Deployment + ClusterIP Service.
46	  # Transport: go-git HTTP (pure-Go, no git binary in tide-push or claude-subagent images).
47	  # Service DNS: http://git-http-server.tide-sample-medium.svc.cluster.local/demo-remote.git
48	  # Short form (same namespace): http://git-http-server/demo-remote.git
49	  targetRepo: http://git-http-server.tide-sample-medium.svc.cluster.local/demo-remote.git
50	
51	  # Medium-sample outcome prompt — small enough to fit comfortably under the
52	  # $5 cap (D-B1) yet substantive enough to exercise TIDE's clone → plan →
53	  # execute → push loop end-to-end. ONE Phase, ONE Plan, target 3-5 tasks
54	  # (tight scope is the cost-control mechanism — same shape as the large
55	  # sample's Variant B per D-B4). Field is `spec.outcomePrompt` (added
56	  # 2026-05-23 root-cause fix; previously carried as annotation in Wave 3
57	  # Plan 05-12 — that workaround is now reverted).
58	  outcomePrompt: |
59	    The targetRepo is a tiny Go scaffold from examples/tide-demo-fixture/
60	    with a `Greeting(name) string` function plus a passing test.
61	
62	    Add a second exported function alongside `Greeting`: `FormattedNow() string`
63	    that returns the current time formatted as RFC3339. Update main.go to
64	    print both functions' output (one per line). Add a unit test for the
65	    new function that asserts the returned string parses back via
66	    time.Parse(time.RFC3339, ...).
67	
68	    Concrete deliverables (tight scope — ONE Phase, ONE Plan, target 3-5
69	    tasks):
70	
71	    1. main.go — add `FormattedNow()`; update main() to print both.
72	    2. main_test.go — add `TestFormattedNow` asserting round-trip parse.
73	
74	    Constraints:
75	    - DO NOT add external dependencies beyond the standard library.
76	    - DO NOT modify go.mod / go.sum.
77	    - Follow the existing repo conventions (MIT license header on new
78	      Go files; deterministic, side-effect-free function shape).
79	
80	    Pass criterion: `go test ./...` is green inside the cloned working tree.
81	
82	  # Phase 2 D-C1..C4: credproxy reads ANTHROPIC_API_KEY from this Secret.
83	  # The Secret is created BEFORE this CRD is applied (README.md step 7).
84	  # Same Secret carries GIT_PAT="" (empty — anonymous in-cluster http:// push
85	  # is accepted by the scheme-conditional guard in cmd/tide-push push mode).
86	  providerSecretRef: tide-secrets
87	
88	  # D-B1: $5 cap for the medium sample. If hit, dispatch halts with
89	  # Phase=BudgetExceeded (Phase 2 D-D2 + Phase 04.1 P4.1 rolling-window
90	  # infra). The tight outcome-prompt scope bounds expected spend well
91	  # under this cap — the cap is a safety net, not the operating budget.
92	  budget:
93	    absoluteCapCents: 500
94	    rollingWindowCapCents: 500
95	    # 24h rolling window per Phase 04.1 P4.1. metav1.Duration parses Go
96	    # duration strings; default would be 24h when unset; explicit per
97	    # Pitfall 7.
98	    rollingWindowDuration: 24h
99	
100	  # Phase 3 D-C2: cost-disciplined model pick per D-B1 spectrum. Haiku 4.5
101	  # is materially cheaper than Sonnet/Opus and adequate for the
102	  # function-addition outcome prompt above. Acceptance test uses Opus
103	  # (large sample); medium uses Haiku.
104	  subagent:
105	    image: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-claude-subagent:1.0.0
106	    model: claude-haiku-4-5
107	
108	  # Phase 3 D-B6 per-project git target. TIDE pushes per-Phase / per-Plan /
109	  # per-Task / final-Project commits to a per-run branch on this remote.
110	  # Branch shape: tide/run-medium-project-<unix-ts>. NEVER pushes to main.
111	  #
112	  # The same `tide-secrets` Secret carries GIT_PAT="" (empty, accepted for
113	  # anonymous http:// remotes by the scheme-conditional guard in tide-push).
114	  git:
115	    repoURL: http://git-http-server.tide-sample-medium.svc.cluster.local/demo-remote.git
116	    credsSecretRef: tide-secrets
117	
118	  # All gates set to `auto` so the medium sample runs end-to-end without
119	  # human interruption. Operators inspecting the run watch
120	  # Project.Status.phase + the dashboard; gate-policy demos are a docs/
121	  # concern, not this sample's purpose.
122	  gates:
123	    milestone: auto
124	    phase: auto
125	    plan: auto
126	    task: auto
127	    pauseBetweenWaves: false
128	
129	  # Phase 2 D-E1: file-touch admission in strict mode. The PLAN.md the
130	  # planner authors must declare its file touches precisely; admission
131	  # rejects on mismatch.
132	  planAdmission:
133	    fileTouchMode: strict
134	
135	  # Phase 2 SUB-04 cap. 3 attempts per task is the v1 default.
136	  maxAttemptsPerTask: 3
137

file.content:
# examples/projects/medium/project.yaml — Phase 5 D-B2 medium sample (~$5 real Claude).
#
# This sample lets operators run TIDE against real Claude (Haiku 4.5, cost-disciplined
# per D-B1) with a hard $5 cap — no external public repo dependency (D-B3). The
# targetRepo is an in-cluster http:// git remote served by the tide-git-http-server
# Deployment (git http-backend CGI + nginx), backed by the demo-remote-pvc populated
# by the demo-remote-init Job. All git transport is pure-Go (go-git HTTP) — no git
# binary in core images required.
#
# APPLY ORDER (do NOT apply this file alone — see README.md):
#   1. namespace.yaml
#   2. demo-remote-pvc.yaml
#   3. per-namespace-resources.yaml      (tide-projects PVC + tide-subagent SA)
#   4. Mirror tide-signing-key Secret from tide-system (see per-namespace-resources.yaml)
#   5. demo-remote-init-job.yaml         (init Job populates the bare repo on demo-remote-pvc)
#      kubectl wait --for=condition=Complete job/demo-remote-init \
#        -n tide-sample-medium --timeout=2m
#   6. git-http-server-deployment.yaml   (after init Job completes — RWO PVC ordering)
#      kubectl wait --for=condition=Available deployment/git-http-server \
#        -n tide-sample-medium --timeout=2m
#   7. kubectl create secret generic tide-secrets \
#        --from-literal=ANTHROPIC_API_KEY="$ANTHROPIC_API_KEY" \
#        --from-literal=GIT_PAT="" \   # empty — anonymous in-cluster http:// push
#        -n tide-sample-medium
#   8. project.yaml                      (THIS file)
#   9. kubectl wait --for=jsonpath='{.status.phase}'=Complete \
#        project/medium-project -n tide-sample-medium --timeout=30m
#
# Schema notes:
#   - ProjectSpec.OutcomePrompt is a first-class Spec field read by the planner subagent.
#   - targetRepo uses http:// in-cluster URL (file:// rejected by CEL validator in Phase 8).
#   - GIT_PAT can be empty — tide-push push mode GIT_PAT guard is scheme-conditional
#     (Phase 8 08-05 Task 3): empty PAT is accepted for anonymous http:// remotes.
---
apiVersion: tideproject.k8s/v1alpha1
kind: Project
metadata:
  name: medium-project
  namespace: tide-sample-medium
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: tide
    app.kubernetes.io/managed-by: tide-sample
    tideproject.k8s/sample: medium
spec:
  # In-cluster anonymous git remote served by git-http-server Deployment + ClusterIP Service.
  # Transport: go-git HTTP (pure-Go, no git binary in tide-push or claude-subagent images).
  # Service DNS: http://git-http-server.tide-sample-medium.svc.cluster.local/demo-remote.git
  # Short form (same namespace): http://git-http-server/demo-remote.git
  targetRepo: http://git-http-server.tide-sample-medium.svc.cluster.local/demo-remote.git

  # Medium-sample outcome prompt — small enough to fit comfortably under the
  # $5 cap (D-B1) yet substantive enough to exercise TIDE's clone → plan →
  # execute → push loop end-to-end. ONE Phase, ONE Plan, target 3-5 tasks
  # (tight scope is the cost-control mechanism — same shape as the large
  # sample's Variant B per D-B4). Field is `spec.outcomePrompt` (added
  # 2026-05-23 root-cause fix; previously carried as annotation in Wave 3
  # Plan 05-12 — that workaround is now reverted).
  outcomePrompt: |
    The targetRepo is a tiny Go scaffold from examples/tide-demo-fixture/
    with a `Greeting(name) string` function plus a passing test.

    Add a second exported function alongside `Greeting`: `FormattedNow() string`
    that returns the current time formatted as RFC3339. Update main.go to
    print both functions' output (one per line). Add a unit test for the
    new function that asserts the returned string parses back via
    time.Parse(time.RFC3339, ...).

    Concrete deliverables (tight scope — ONE Phase, ONE Plan, target 3-5
    tasks):

    1. main.go — add `FormattedNow()`; update main() to print both.
    2. main_test.go — add `TestFormattedNow` asserting round-trip parse.

    Constraints:
    - DO NOT add external dependencies beyond the standard library.
    - DO NOT modify go.mod / go.sum.
    - Follow the existing repo conventions (MIT license header on new
      Go files; deterministic, side-effect-free function shape).

    Pass criterion: `go test ./...` is green inside the cloned working tree.

  # Phase 2 D-C1..C4: credproxy reads ANTHROPIC_API_KEY from this Secret.
  # The Secret is created BEFORE this CRD is applied (README.md step 7).
  # Same Secret carries GIT_PAT="" (empty — anonymous in-cluster http:// push
  # is accepted by the scheme-conditional guard in cmd/tide-push push mode).
  providerSecretRef: tide-secrets

  # D-B1: $5 cap for the medium sample. If hit, dispatch halts with
  # Phase=BudgetExceeded (Phase 2 D-D2 + Phase 04.1 P4.1 rolling-window
  # infra). The tight outcome-prompt scope bounds expected spend well
  # under this cap — the cap is a safety net, not the operating budget.
  budget:
    absoluteCapCents: 500
    rollingWindowCapCents: 500
    # 24h rolling window per Phase 04.1 P4.1. metav1.Duration parses Go
    # duration strings; default would be 24h when unset; explicit per
    # Pitfall 7.
    rollingWindowDuration: 24h

  # Phase 3 D-C2: cost-disciplined model pick per D-B1 spectrum. Haiku 4.5
  # is materially cheaper than Sonnet/Opus and adequate for the
  # function-addition outcome prompt above. Acceptance test uses Opus
  # (large sample); medium uses Haiku.
  subagent:
    image: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-claude-subagent:1.0.0
    model: claude-haiku-4-5

  # Phase 3 D-B6 per-project git target. TIDE pushes per-Phase / per-Plan /
  # per-Task / final-Project commits to a per-run branch on this remote.
  # Branch shape: tide/run-medium-project-<unix-ts>. NEVER pushes to main.
  #
  # The same `tide-secrets` Secret carries GIT_PAT="" (empty, accepted for
  # anonymous http:// remotes by the scheme-conditional guard in tide-push).
  git:
    repoURL: http://git-http-server.tide-sample-medium.svc.cluster.local/demo-remote.git
    credsSecretRef: tide-secrets

  # All gates set to `auto` so the medium sample runs end-to-end without
  # human interruption. Operators inspecting the run watch
  # Project.Status.phase + the dashboard; gate-policy demos are a docs/
  # concern, not this sample's purpose.
  gates:
    milestone: auto
    phase: auto
    plan: auto
    task: auto
    pauseBetweenWaves: false

  # Phase 2 D-E1: file-touch admission in strict mode. The PLAN.md the
  # planner authors must declare its file touches precisely; admission
  # rejects on mismatch.
  planAdmission:
    fileTouchMode: strict

  # Phase 2 SUB-04 cap. 3 attempts per task is the v1 default.
  maxAttemptsPerTask: 3

11	distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
12	WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
13	See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
14	limitations under the License.

16	
17	package dispatch
18	
19	// ProviderSpec selects the LLM vendor + model + per-vendor tuning knobs the
20	// subagent image should use to satisfy a single dispatch (D-C3). It is carried
21	// on every EnvelopeIn so vendor + model are orthogonal axes from the
22	// orchestrator's point of view: the dispatching reconciler resolves
23	// Project.Spec.subagent.levels.{level}.{vendor,model,params} → Project-level
24	// defaults → Helm-chart defaults and stamps the resolved triple here. The
25	// provider-side Subagent implementation (e.g. internal/subagent/anthropic)
26	// reads Provider.Model to drive its CLI invocation and rejects an envelope
27	// whose Provider.Vendor does not match the image's compiled-in sentinel —
28	// fail-fast defense against config drift between image tag and envelope
29	// content.
30	//
31	// Params is intentionally a string→string map (not a typed struct) so future
32	// per-vendor knobs (Anthropic thinking-budget, OpenAI temperature, Google
33	// safetySettings, etc.) can land without a pkg/dispatch schema bump every
34	// time. Validation of Params keys/values is the provider implementation's
35	// responsibility — pkg/dispatch only guarantees the bytes round-trip.
36	type ProviderSpec struct {
37		// Vendor is the provider sentinel string the subagent image checks at
38		// startup. Canonical values: "anthropic", "openai", "google", "xai",
39		// "opencode". Required (no omitempty) — every dispatch declares a vendor.
40		Vendor string `json:"vendor"`
41	
42		// Model is the per-vendor model identifier passed to the provider CLI or
43		// SDK (e.g. "claude-opus-4-7", "claude-sonnet-4-6", "claude-haiku-4-5",
44		// "o1-mini"). Required (no omitempty).
45		Model string `json:"model"`
46	
47		// Params is the per-vendor tuning passthrough. Keys and values are
48		// vendor-specific; pkg/dispatch performs no semantic validation. Omitted
49		// from JSON when empty so dispatches that pass no tuning don't carry a
50		// "params: {}" placeholder.
51		Params map[string]string `json:"params,omitempty"`
52	}
53

2	Copyright 2026 TIDE Authors.
3	
4	Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
